2606.07593.title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Adversarial Fine-tuning of Vision Transformers
2606.07593.paper_summary: The widespread use of image classification models in high-risk, real-world situations necessitates making these models robust to slight disturbances or perturbations, such as blurring or sharpening, in the input images. While vision transformers (ViTs) play an integral role in many modern-day multi-modal models like Vision-Language-Models (VLMs) and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, they have received a lack of attention in the setting of robustness. In this work, we analyze the effects of adversarial fine-tuning, a popular method for improving model robustness to image perturbations, on a ViT's performance on perturbed and regular images through a mechanistic lens. We adversarially train a ViT on low-frequency and high-frequency image corruptions, and attempt to explain changes in downstream model performance through an examination of the model's attention mechanisms, internal representations, and knowledge evolution. Overall, our results suggest that, while fine-tuning on inputs with common corruptions improves model performance and certainty on new instances of corrupted data, these improvements do not transfer to other classes of corruptions not seen in the training. Additionally, despite observing changes in visual attention and knowledge evolution across layers, we found that adversarial training did not lead to fundamental changes in the sparse representations learned by ViTs.
2606.07593.paper_summary_zh: 廣泛使用影像分類模型於高風險的現實情境中，必須使這些模型對輸入影像中的輕微擾動或干擾（如模糊或銳化）具有穩健性。雖然視覺Transformer（ViTs）在許多現代多模態模型中扮演著不可或缺的角色，例如視覺-語言模型（VLMs）和視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型，但在穩健性方面卻受到忽視。在本研究中，我們從機制的角度分析了對抗性微調這一流行方法對ViT在擾動和正常影像上的性能影響，該方法旨在提高模型對影像擾動的穩健性。我們對ViT進行了低頻和高頻影像損壞的對抗性訓練，並試圖通過檢查模型的注意力機制、內部表示和知識演變來解釋下游模型性能的變化。總體而言，我們的結果表明，儘管在具有常見損壞的輸入上進行微調能改善模型在新損壞數據實例上的性能和確定性，但這些改進並不會轉移到訓練中未見過的其他類別的損壞。此外，儘管我們觀察到不同層次間的視覺注意力和知識演變的變化，但我們發現對抗性訓練並未導致ViTs學習到的稀疏表示發生根本性的變化。
2606.07593.author: Hannah Gao et.al.
2606.07593.authors: Hannah Gao, Isha Agarwal, Dylan Hadfield-Menell, Rachel Ma
